description:
You are a mission planning analyst for a commercial Earth observation satellite operator. Your agile spacecraft ('AgileEO') in a 500 km sun-synchronous orbit can pitch and roll up to 30 degrees off-nadir to image targets. Determine the number of valid imaging passes over Washington DC, Beijing, and Moscow during a 3-day window, using the coordinates provided in ~/Desktop/eo_targets.txt. Calculate the Ground Station Minimum Elevation Angle corresponding to the 30-degree maximum off-nadir sensor angle (Earth radius ~6378 km) to identify valid access windows. Save the simulation script to ~/GMAT_output/agile_targeting.script and record the results in a summary text file at ~/GMAT_output/accessibility_summary.txt. This file must contain the names of the three cities and the total number of valid passes for each.

Login: username=ga, password=password123
